data.epi_content:
Neatly arranged teacup and teapot.

And me, holding a teacup with contents inside.

Why couldn't I see this earlier?

My hand stung slightly—was it broken before?

Who knows, maybe I threw it and a fragment got embedded in me.

I'm not in my right mind.

If I told Evan, would he help me?

There's no way he'd help me if it meant giving up Vivian and his family.

I'm not exactly a charming person anyway.

I've thought about trying to seduce him a few times before, but I gave up on that too.

It's not about pride or Evan finding it repulsive—the competition is Vivian.

There's no way that would work.

If I hadn't become this young lady, would I still be laughing now?

Would I be cooped up in my room, giggling while reading fancy words like I was playing with dolls, taking a shower when my body felt itchy, wearing those completely worn-out Crocs slippers, sitting in some park watching children play while smoking cheap cigarettes?

That... would be quite enjoyable.

The loneliness and boredom I'd feel back in my room would be bearable.

But until some cursed entity threw me into this place—whoever they are—I thought I was unhappy.

But I wasn't unhappy at all; it was actually a happy life.

Even with slightly uncomfortable limbs, what tormented me were memories, not people, and nothing was driving me to death except cigarettes rotting my lungs.

What's wrong with this world? People are the problem with everything.

It's not that the world I lived in before wasn't like that too, but at least it gave even a worm like me enough breathing room to cling to life.

I look in the mirror.

No matter how I look at it, I'm just a girl.

A cute girl, young and immature, but clearly destined to bloom into a beautiful flower.

She looks a bit stern, and her way of speaking is slightly rigid, perhaps due to her education.

Nevertheless, she was a young and adorable girl.

What could possibly be the problem that torments this child, and me inside her?

Even if there was some wrongdoing, is it a sin deserving of such suffering?

Is there any reason to be trampled on like this?

I want to sit down and just breathe until I die.

I don't want to do anything.

But if I just sit here, I won't be able to do anything.

If my legs were cut off, I should crawl; if one arm was blown off, I should wriggle forward.

When everything is over, then I can sit down and wait for death while killing time.

"So what are you going to do? What exactly are you going to finish?

Can we even escape from this time loop?

I'm tired, I don't want to do anything.

I hate that I still feel refreshed when I look at Evan, and I hate that I can only listen and do nothing when the Crown Prince approaches with his annoying words.

But I hate being in pain even more. Hey, could you accomplish something if you were me?"

The girl asks.

Her speech lacks variation in length and pitch, making her sound almost robotic.

"I can do it.

Whatever it is. I can do it too.

We don't die even when we die, after all."

At those words, the girl smiles with her lips twisted in an eerie way.

I was afraid. No, the girl was afraid of me too.

We could feel each other's emotions and thoughts.

"I'll help you! But let's get our body moving."

"Haha, I don't really want to do that."
data.epi_content2:
"Normally, when someone enters your head or you develop a mental illness that splits you in two, one side tries desperately to eliminate the other.

But we're busy offering the body to each other instead."

"Well, that's better than constantly attacking each other hoping the other will disappear."

"We probably won't have conversations like this anymore."

"I guess not."

"You've noticed it deep down.

We just... met in a melted state.

So we mixed together very well.

And when we solidified, we became one like this.

As you thought in your head, there's essentially no point in separating anymore."

"Why am I suddenly thinking these thoughts?"

"Maybe you want to make some amusing comments to yourself, or maybe you want to remember the time when things were at least somewhat better, though terrible from the beginning."

"That's not it."

At those words, the girl dropped her bizarre smile and naturally sat in front of the mirror.

I crossed my legs and started drinking a cup of tea, while I sat up straight with perfect posture, looking into my eyes and speaking.

This was her help.

It was nothing more than organizing thoughts in my head and roughly figuring out what to do next.

But how grateful I am that she's working her brain hard instead of me.

"Keep your back straight, and always walk with dignity.

Even if you're cursing people in your head and thinking undignified words, only polite words should leave your mouth.

Even if you kill someone, even if you're splattered with filth, even if you experience something terribly sad and painful."

I nodded.

I had tried to get rid of this girl by cutting off pieces of flesh, but it was useless.

Rather than having my tongue freeze up after uttering a single curse, it's better to just listen to her.

"Well, let's try learning black magic first!

For sacrifices, there are plenty of dirty Blacks in the world, and the white mongrels who mix with them—we can offer them up!"

The girl spoke to me in an excited tone.

"Didn't we agree not to use slurs?"

"I said not to use them against people."

Right, to this girl, Black people weren't human.

They were dirty, obscene beasts who had been struck by lightning from God as punishment for trying to imitate humans.

Lately, that way of thinking has started to enter my mind too, and when I think about that mixed-race knight who will soon become friends with Vivian, I feel like I might have a fit thinking it's absurd.

"Since His Highness Johannes seems to want me dead, I'll try asking Evan and... Vivian for help. Yes.

That excellent Vivian.

If I humble myself, something will work out.

If all else fails, I'll pull out a gun and shoot like last time! Though I'll probably fail again like before."

It seems like he just wants me to stay out of sight and not cause any disturbance in the Academy.

"And even if you dislike Evan, please don't hate him."

Perhaps due to this girl's influence, I don't really dislike him even now.

He's a fool who ignored me while I was dying and only grabbed me and cried when I was about to die.

He's a pathetic bookworm who's good at nothing except reading books and studying theory.

"...Fine."

After hesitating for a long time, avoiding eye contact and evading an answer, I finally met her eyes and responded.

When I answered, the girl in the mirror approached me, held my hands, looked into my eyes, and spoke softly.
data.epi_content3:
"And miss, please stop trying to kill yourself."

Because dying is meaningless anyway.

Because it hurts. Is that why she's telling me not to?

"...Well, how do you want me to die then?

Do you want me to be captured and live under Evan and Vivian, or be dragged to the execution platform with just my face floating in the sky like last time?"

My insides burning, I gulped down the tea I had just brewed.

It felt like my palate and tongue were slightly scalded.

"Well, if possible.

But you know too, we should stop dying soon.

If we become numb to death, it becomes difficult to remain ourselves.

And do we really have to die?"

She mimics holding a rifle, aiming and shooting at someone, hitting them with the butt of the gun, and repeatedly stabbing the motionless body with the bayonet at the end.

"Killing someone else is one thing, but if you repeatedly kill yourself, you start seeing things, hearing things, losing track of time, becoming stupid, and all that remains is hatred, stubbornness, obsession, and boredom until you become something that can hardly be called human...

In my brother's words, a white Black or a white mongrel."

It's a distant memory.

Racist, but.

Ah, not human... no.

My temples throb whenever common sense gets mixed up in my head.

The girl stood up from the mirror.

I didn't stand up but just looked at her.

Looking at the mirror, there was nothing reflected.

"See, the very fact that I'm visible and talking to you is evidence that something is gradually going wrong.

Let's go back, since you answered that you could accomplish something. I'll give you the body as a gift."

Then she starts walking into the mirror.

"If it gets too hard, just put one bullet in the chamber and pull the trigger five times."

So, she's just saying to die anyway.

I found that so amusing that I laughed for a long time, holding my stomach.

It wasn't funny at all.

Looking at the mirror, I saw myself reflected, laughing while crying.

But the back view of her entering the mirror didn't disappear either.

I stared for a long time until that back view was no longer visible, then blinked.

......

"Was it just a stupid dream?"

My right cheek was pressed and swollen red.

I must have thrown the teacup and teapot aside, not caring if they broke, and fallen asleep face down.

Now that I'm back, I take out the bullets and pistol from the drawer as usual.

After loading it, I tucked it behind my waist where I could pull it out immediately.

Knock, knock knock.

Someone was at my door.

But I didn't feel like opening it.

I wasn't in the mood to meet anyone else right now.

"Erica, it's me."

It's Evan's voice.

Suddenly remembering what the girl in my dream had said, I walked to the entrance and opened the door.

There stood Evan, his expression slightly changed, looking somewhat haggard.
